acceptance_criteria: Backend deployment posture is expressed as wasmCloud on Wasmtime with deployable component metadata instead of native service binaries.
The OS shell subset is rehomed under ui/ and exposes only the governed release apps for this product profile.
SurrealDB access is centralized behind shared repository abstractions and UI data access stays on typed SDK/contracts.
Workspace tooling, CI, and process validation encode the new ui and wasmCloud-first rules.

open_questions.question: Nomad host/service topology can be refined further once real lattice rollout automation is introduced.

objective: Review the current workspace, imported OS crates, and deployment/runtime assumptions.
findings: The repository already assumes Rust-first services and wasmCloud-compatible workloads but did not yet encode component metadata and ui ownership uniformly.
The OS shell can be rehomed into ui/ with a trimmed release surface and typed host/runtime contracts.
SurrealDB access needed a shared repository layer to make the no-direct-ui-db rule enforceable.
constraints: Avoid breaking canonical contracts outside schemas.
Keep browser/WASM preview available while treating Tauri desktop as the release runtime.
